instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Download Form 26AS through TRACES
In a financial year, there are several tax deductions from an account. As a taxpayer, you, too, may be curious to get this consolidated detail in one place rather than digging through various websites and documents. Form 26AS is the document you may need for this. Form 26AS is a comprehensive document containing all the year's tax-related information. It shows details based on the linked PAN card.¹
Not only does it provide you an insight into taxes, but it is also essential when it comes to tax filing. It is quite easy and simple to download Form 26AS as and when required. Read on as we discuss the importance of Form 26AS and how you can download it.
What is Form 26AS?
Form 26AS is an official statement document that mentions all the tax-related transactions linked with a PAN Card. It also provides information on advance tax and self-assessment tax payments, along with details of any high-value transactions. You can easily download Form 26AS from the TRACES website.¹
Form 26AS can also be viewed when you wish to know if the TDS deducted from your account was deposited to the income tax department or not.¹ The form contains transactions like TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) and/ or TCS (Tax Collected at Source).
The updated form now also contains details of mutual fund purchases, foreign remittances, refund details, dividends, etc.¹
What does Form 26AS contain?
Form 26AS majorly contains tax-related transaction details of a PAN Card. The following details are usually in the Form 26AS:¹
TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) transaction details
Details of TCS (Tax Collected at Source) transactions
Any advance tax that is paid
Tax payment by self-assessment
Regular assessment tax paid
All the details of the refund received from the income tax on the PAN Card in a financial year
Any major transaction like shares purchase or sale, mutual fund transactions, etc.
Tax deducted on sale of any immovable property
TDS defaults in the financial year
Details of turnover as mentioned in GSTR-3B
Any specified financial transaction
Completed and pending proceedings of Income tax.
Structure of Form 26AS: 2022-2023 onwards
As discussed above, the form now also contains details of mutual funds, dividends, foreign remittances, etc. Here is a glance at the different structures in Form 26AS:¹
Part I
Transaction information of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on business, interest income, salary, profession, etc.¹
Part II
Transaction description of Tax Deducted at Source (15G/15H). This is usually for senior citizens who have income less than the exemption limit.¹
Part III
This part has transaction details under Proviso to the IT section 194B/First Proviso to its sub-section (1) of the IT section 194R/ Proviso to its sub-section(1) of IT section 194S.¹
It also collects details of TDS on payments done in kind like lottery prizes, etc.
Part IV
TDS details under Sections 194IA/ 194IB / 194M/ 194S (Property's landlord/Seller/Professionals/Contractors/ Seller of Virtual Digital Asset).¹
TDS is deducted if the house rent exceeds ₹50,000 per month, payment is made to a professional or contractor above ₹50 lakhs, and virtual digital assets like cryptocurrency transactions. Cryptocurrency transactions attract 1% TDS, and for those without a PAN card, TDS is 20%².
Part V
Part V has transaction details under Proviso to sub-section(1) of the IT section 194S as per the Form-26QE  Virtual Digital Asset seller)¹.
Part VI
Any TCS transaction details under Section 206C¹.
Part VII
Part VIII has details of refunds¹.
Part VIII
This part has TDS details applicable under Section 194IA/ 194IB /194M/194S (Concerned with the buyer of a property, tenant, payments made to contractors/professionals, or buyers of virtual digital assets like cryptocurrency)¹.
Part IX
This part has transaction/demand payment details under Proviso to sub-section(1) of section 194S as per Form 26QE¹.
Part X
Default payments for TDS/TCS¹.
Steps to download Form 26AS
Taxpayers can access Form 26AS to view or download it either through the TRACES platform. To download Form 26AS, you need to follow the following steps¹:
Step 1
Visit the official website of TRACES.
Step 2
Enter log in details, which are usually the PAN or UID number or any other user ID created by you. Enter the password and continue.
Step 3
On the screen, click on e-file and then choose the option ‘Income Tax Returns’.
Step 4
Here, click on the drop-down menu and choose ‘Form 26AS’.
Step 5
Now, click on “Confirm,” after which you will be redirected to the TRACES website.
Step 6
On the TRACES website, click “Proceed” on the box that appears.
Step 7
Scroll to the end of the page and click on the ‘View Tax Credit (Form 26AS)’. It will show you your Form 26AS.
Step 8
Next, enter the year of assessment you want Form 26AS for and the format. To download it, choose pdf format.
Step 9
Submit the verification code and click on the ‘View/Download’ option.
Once downloaded, Form 26AS can be viewed or you can print it for further requirements.
Conclusion
Form 26AS is an essential document for a taxpayer. Whether you want to check the update of your TDS or TCS payment or you need it for tax filing, Form 26AS plays a crucial role. By following the simple steps, you can easily download or view the Form 26AS associated with your PAN Card.
FAQs
What is Form 26AS?
Form 26AS is a document that contains details of the tax-related transaction associated with a PAN Card. It contains details of TCS and TDS payments along with high-value transactions like cryptocurrency, mutual funds, dividends, etc.
Can I view Form 26AS through net banking?
Yes, several banks allow you to view Form 26AS through their Internet banking facility. The list of these banks is: ¹
Citibank N.A.
The Saraswat Co-operative Bank Limited
State Bank of Patiala
UCO Bank
Bank of India
Axis Bank Limited
Oriental Bank of Commerce
IDBI Bank Limited
Indian Overseas Bank
State Bank of Travancore
City Union Bank Limited
Corporation Bank
Karnataka Bank
The Federal Bank Limited
Union Bank of India
State Bank of India
Kotak Mahindra Bank Limited
Bank of Baroda
Indian Bank
ICICI Bank Limited
Bank of Maharashtra
State Bank of Mysore.
References:
https://cleartax.in/s/view-form-26as-tax-credit-statement
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/tax/how-tds-on-crypto-virtual-digital-assets-will-work-from-july-1-2022/articleshow/92409625.cms
Disclaimer
The views expressed in this article is not to be construed as professional advice and users are advised to seek independent professional/expert advice before making any decisions based on the same. This part has transaction/demand payment details under Proviso to sub-section(1) of section 194S as per Form 26QE¹. ● Part X Default payments for TDS/TCS¹.
